---
title: "{{< meta _template.title >}}"
subtitle: "Standards & Requirements Specification"
author:
  - name: "{{< var author.name >}}"
    affiliation: "{{< var author.affiliation >}}"
date: last-modified
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
version: "{{< var doc.version >}}"
status: "{{< var doc.status >}}"

# --- Template Parameters (override in _variables.yml or per-document) ---
params:
  doc_version: "1.0"
  doc_status: "Draft"

# --- Output Formats ---
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    theme: cosmo
    code-fold: true
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    papersize: letter
    fontsize: 11pt
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
  docx:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
  gfm:
    toc: true

# --- Cross-reference configuration ---
crossref:
  tbl-prefix: "Table"
  fig-prefix: "Figure"
---

<!-- 
================================================================================
REQUIREMENTS DOCUMENT TEMPLATE
================================================================================
This is a reusable Quarto template for writing formal requirements specifications.

INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Copy this file and rename it for your specific requirements document.
2. Replace all placeholders wrapped in {{ PLACEHOLDER }} with your content.
3. Add/remove requirement rows as needed in each category table.
4. Update the evaluation criteria if your scoring methodology differs.
5. Render via: quarto render <filename>.qmd --to html,pdf,docx
================================================================================
-->

# Purpose {#sec-purpose}

<!-- Describe what this requirements document is for. What problem/decision will it inform? -->

{{ PURPOSE_DESCRIPTION }}

---

# Requirements Classification {#sec-classification}

Requirements are classified into three tiers:

| Tier | Label | Weight | Meaning |
|------|-------|--------|---------|
| **R** | Required | Must-have | Candidate is disqualified without this capability |
| **SP** | Strongly Preferred | High priority | Significant penalty for absence |
| **P** | Preferred | Nice-to-have | Positive differentiator when present |

---

# Requirements {#sec-requirements}

<!-- 
  Organize requirements into logical categories.
  Each category gets its own subsection with a table of requirements.
  Copy/paste the category template below as needed.
-->

## {{ CATEGORY_1_NAME }} {#sec-cat-1}

<!-- Brief description of what this category covers. -->

| ID | Tier | Requirement |
|----|------|-------------|
| **R-1** | **{{ TIER }}** | {{ REQUIREMENT_DESCRIPTION }} |
| **R-2** | **{{ TIER }}** | {{ REQUIREMENT_DESCRIPTION }} |

## {{ CATEGORY_2_NAME }} {#sec-cat-2}

| ID | Tier | Requirement |
|----|------|-------------|
| **R-3** | **{{ TIER }}** | {{ REQUIREMENT_DESCRIPTION }} |
| **R-4** | **{{ TIER }}** | {{ REQUIREMENT_DESCRIPTION }} |

<!-- Add more categories as needed following the same pattern. -->

---

# Evaluation Criteria {#sec-evaluation}

Each candidate will be scored on a **0–3** scale per requirement:

| Score | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| **3** | Fully supported natively |
| **2** | Supported with minimal configuration or first-party plugin |
| **1** | Achievable via third-party OSS tools/chaining |
| **0** | Not supported or prohibitively difficult |

## Weighted Scoring

Scores are multiplied by tier weight:

| Tier | Multiplier |
|------|------------|
| Required (**R**) | ×3 |
| Strongly Preferred (**SP**) | ×2 |
| Preferred (**P**) | ×1 |

**Maximum possible score:** {{ CALCULATED_MAX_SCORE }}

<!-- Formula: Σ (count_per_tier × tier_weight × max_score_per_req) -->

---

# Known Candidates {#sec-candidates}

<!-- List pre-identified candidates for evaluation. -->

1. **{{ CANDIDATE_1 }}** — {{ SHORT_DESCRIPTION }}
2. **{{ CANDIDATE_2 }}** — {{ SHORT_DESCRIPTION }}

<!-- Additional candidates will be identified through research. -->

---

# Deliverables {#sec-deliverables}

<!-- What artifacts will be produced from this requirements process? -->

1. **This document** — Formal requirements specification
2. {{ ADDITIONAL_DELIVERABLE }}
